Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single shoe product available for sale in the store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the shoe product.

price.description: The price of the shoe product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

imageUrl.description: The URL of the primary image for the product, typically stored in Firebase Storage.

galleryImageUrls.description: An array of URLs for additional images of the product, from the media library.

stockStatus.description: The current stock status of the product. Possible values: 'In Stock', 'Out of Stock', 'Few Left'.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, potentially generated by AI.

slug.description: A URL-friendly, unique identifier derived from the product name, used for SEO and clean URLs.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category of shoes (e.g., Sneakers, Boots) for organizing products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly, unique identifier derived from the category name.

coverImageUrl.description: The URL of an image representing the category, often used on landing pages.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order placed through the WhatsApp checkout process.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order, if logged in. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

customerName.description: The name provided by the customer for delivery.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The phone number provided by the customer for delivery.

deliveryLocation.description: The delivery address or location provided by the customer.

specialNotes.description: Any special instructions or notes from the customer regarding delivery.

items.description: A list of products included in the order.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

orderStatus.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

orderedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

whatsappMessageSent.description: Indicates whether the WhatsApp order message was successfully generated and sent/opened by the customer.

receiptPdfUrl.description: Optional URL to a generated PDF receipt for the order.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by an administrator regarding this order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product item within an order, capturing its state at the time of order.

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

productName.description: The name of the product at the time the order was placed.

productPrice.description: The price of the product at the time the order was placed.

quantity.description: The quantity of this product ordered.

productImageUrl.description: The image URL of the product at the time the order was placed, for historical reference.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents additional profile information for users authenticated via Firebase Auth, including their role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: The role of the user within the application. Possible values: 'customer', 'admin'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

MediaItem.title: MediaItem
MediaItem.description: Represents an image file stored in Firebase Storage, part of the reusable media library for admins.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaItem entity.

url.description: The public URL to access the image in Firebase Storage.

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded image.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (admin) who uploaded this media item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MediaItem)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the image was uploaded.

description.description: An optional description or alt text for the image.

definition.description: Main collection for all product listings. Publicly readable for the storefront, with admin-only write access for management. No denormalized authorization fields as it's public data.
Collection for organizing products into categories. Publicly readable for the storefront, with admin-only write access. No denormalized authorization fields as it's public data.

Stores user-specific data including roles (customer/admin) linked to Firebase Auth UID. Owner can read/write their own profile; administrators can read/write all. The 'role' field is critical for DBAC (Database Access Control).

Stores individual customer orders, nested under their `UserProfile` for strong ownership and simplified authorization. Includes denormalized `userId` matching the parent path for explicit rule checking.

Details of products within a specific order, stored as a subcollection. Critically includes denormalized product details (`productName`, `productPrice`, `productImageUrl`) at the time of purchase for historical accuracy, ensuring Authorization Independence from the main `/products` collection for past orders.

Centralized media library for admin-uploaded images. Only accessible by administrators (checked via their `UserProfile` role). Includes `uploadedByUserId` for auditing, but authorization relies on the caller's admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
1.  **User Profiles and Roles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored at `/userProfiles/{userId}`, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This path-based authorization ensures that a user can only read/write their own profile data, making rules extremely simple (`request.auth.uid == userId`). The `role` field (e.g., 'customer', 'admin') is denormalized directly onto this document, enabling authorization checks like `get(/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'admin'` without relying on complex, non-atomic cross-document lookups for other data operations. This is crucial for DBAC (Database Access Control) as roles are stored in the database, not custom claims.
2.  **User-Owned Orders (`/userProfiles/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`):** Orders are stored as a subcollection under the specific user's profile. This hierarchy (`/userProfiles/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`) inherently enforces ownership. A user can only access orders under their own `userId` path. The `userId` field is also explicitly present in the `Order` document, reinforcing this ownership and allowing for direct rule checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`) for robustness, even though the path already guarantees it. This eliminates `get()` calls to other user data to determine ownership.
3.  **Order Items (`/userProfiles/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`):** Each item within an order is further nested. This maintains the strong ownership hierarchy and allows `OrderItem` details to be accessed in the context of a specific user's order. Crucially, `OrderItem` documents denormalize product-specific details like `productName`, `productPrice`, and `productImageUrl`. This is vital for historical accuracy; an order item's details remain consistent even if the original product catalog changes.

**QAPs (Query/Access Patterns) Support & Structural Segregation:**
1.  **Public Product Catalog (`/products` and `/categories`):** These collections are top-level, allowing efficient and unfiltered `list` operations for all users, supporting the display of new arrivals, categories, and product cards without security rule filtering. Public read access is granted, while write access is restricted to authenticated administrators.
2.  **User-Specific Order History (`/userProfiles/{userId}/orders`):** By nesting orders under `userProfiles`, users can only list and access their *own* orders, which directly supports the requirement for users to see
